title: Albert S. Evans

infobox.value: was an American explorer and writer. Prior to 1856, he lived in [Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania](/wiki/Pittsburgh,_Pennsylvania "Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania") and worked as a broker.[[2]](#cite_note-Pittsburg_Post-2) He lived in [Chicago](/wiki/Chicago "Chicago") and worked for many years at the *Daily Journal*. Evans arrived in [San Francisco](/wiki/San_Francisco "San Francisco") in 1861, began working as a journalist for the *Morning Call*[[3]](#cite_note-Arizona_Miner-3) and served on the staff of the Governor of California during the [Civil War](/wiki/American_Civil_War "American Civil War").[[2]](#cite_note-Pittsburg_Post-2)

["January 16, 1865: First Edition of San Francisco's Daily Dramatic Chronicle"](https://web.archive.org/web/20160304042607/http://friendsofcalarchives.org/2014/01/january-16-1865-first-edition-of-the-daily-dramatic-chronicle/). *Friends of the California Archives*. 2014-01-16. Archived from [the original](http://friendsofcalarchives.org/2014/01/january-16-1865-first-edition-of-the-daily-dramatic-chronicle/) on March 4, 2016.

["Water and Flame"](https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2239069/water_and_flame_the_new_york_herald/). New York, New York: The New York Herald. 31 October 1872. Retrieved 18 April 2015 – via [Newspapers.com](/wiki/Newspapers.com "Newspapers.com"). ["Daily Alta California 2 November 1872 — California Digital Newspaper Collection"](http://cdnc.ucr.edu/cgi-bin/cdnc?a=d&d=DAC18721102.2.39).

Evans arrived in [San Francisco](/wiki/San_Francisco "San Francisco") in 1861, began working as a journalist for the *Morning Call*[[3]](#cite_note-Arizona_Miner-3) and served on the staff of the Governor of California during the [Civil War](/wiki/American_Civil_War "American Civil War").[[2]](#cite_note-Pittsburg_Post-2)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

| Colonel   Albert S. Evans | |
| --- | --- |
| [Colonel Albert S. Evans, from A La California](/wiki/File:Col._Albert_S._Evans.jpg "Colonel Albert S. Evans, from A La California") Colonel Albert S. Evans, from *A La California* | |
| Died | (1872-10-22)22 October 1872 |
| Pen name | Fitz Smythe[[1]](#cite_note-chronicle-1) |

**Albert S. Evans** was an American explorer and writer. Prior to 1856, he lived in [Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania](/wiki/Pittsburgh,_Pennsylvania "Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania") and worked as a broker.[[2]](#cite_note-Pittsburg_Post-2) He lived in [Chicago](/wiki/Chicago "Chicago") and worked for many years at the *Daily Journal*. Evans arrived in [San Francisco](/wiki/San_Francisco "San Francisco") in 1861, began working as a journalist for the *Morning Call*[[3]](#cite_note-Arizona_Miner-3) and served on the staff of the Governor of California during the [Civil War](/wiki/American_Civil_War "American Civil War").[[2]](#cite_note-Pittsburg_Post-2)

He published two travelogues, *Our Sister Republic: A Gala Trip through Tropical Mexico in 1869 - 1870*, (1870) and *Á La California: Sketches of Life in the Golden State* (1873). The books were published by A. L. Bancroft of [San Francisco](/wiki/San_Francisco "San Francisco"), the publishing partner and brother of [Hubert Howe Bancroft](/wiki/Hubert_Howe_Bancroft "Hubert Howe Bancroft").[[4]](#cite_note-Twain_Quotes-4) In 1863, Evans became local editor for some years of *[The Daily Alta California](/wiki/The_Daily_Alta_California "The Daily Alta California")* in San Francisco, and continued in that capacity for several years.[[3]](#cite_note-Arizona_Miner-3) He lived in the city for 12 years, and he famously feuded with [Mark Twain](/wiki/Mark_Twain "Mark Twain") when both were in the city.[[4]](#cite_note-Twain_Quotes-4) Evans died 22 October 1872,[[5]](#cite_note-Herald_(1872)-5) a passenger on the steamship *Missouri*, when it burned at sea.[[6]](#cite_note-6)
